data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-dc157075">
    <title>ddx work / agent execute-loop: stay alive while queue has ready work and viable providers exist</title>
    <description>
Workers exit prematurely even when ready beads remain in the queue. Operators have to manually relaunch, defeating the point of a long-running drain.

## Root causes (from sub-agent investigation)

1. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:440-441**: when nextCandidate returns no eligible bead AND runtime.PollInterval <= 0, the loop returns immediately. Combined with the per-Run 'attempted' map (line 388, 476), once each currently-ready bead has been *attempted once* the loop exits even though those same beads (or new arrivals) are still queue-ready.

2. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:483-508**: routing-preflight rejection on a single bead exits the entire loop ('return result, nil'), abandoning all other ready beads.

3. **Default --poll-interval=0 everywhere**: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. All three recent server-spawned workers in .ddx/workers/ show poll_interval: '0s', so cause #1 fires the first empty poll.

## Fix shape (locked decisions baked in)

- Flip default --poll-interval from 0 → **30s** everywhere (CLI + server adapter). Operator can override with --once for one-shot or explicit --poll-interval=N.
- Reset per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps when the loop falls through to 'no candidate', before sleeping. Prevents the 'attempted-once = exit' trap.
- Convert preflight early-exit (483-508) to per-bead 'continue' so one bead's routing failure doesn't abandon the rest.
- Add structured exit_reason to loop.end event; reserve 'providers_exhausted' slot for ddx-aede917d (quota-pause work).
- Server-managed workers expose 'no_ready_work' as a **transient 'running (idle)' substate**, not terminal. Worker stays alive; UI shows running (idle).
- Preserve --once and explicit --poll-interval=0 for back-compat (legacy 'drain-and-exit' semantics).

Acceptable exit conditions remain (per existing beads):
- SIGTERM / SIGINT (operator-driven)
- All providers durably out (per ddx-aede917d quota pause/resume — workers pause+probe, not exit)
- No providers configured at all (fatal config error)
- --once explicitly requested
- --poll-interval=0 explicitly requested
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Default --poll-interval = 30s in all four call sites: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. 2. execute_bead_loop.go:440-441 no longer returns early when poll-interval > 0; sleeps + retries instead. Per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps reset on no-candidate path. 3. execute_bead_loop.go:483-508 preflight rejection becomes per-bead continue, not loop exit. 4. loop.end event includes structured exit_reason field. Recognized values include 'sigterm', 'sigint', 'fatal_config', 'once_complete', 'explicit_poll_zero'; 'providers_exhausted' slot reserved for ddx-aede917d. 5. Server-managed workers report no_ready_work as transient (worker stays in 'running' state with 'idle' substate); existing 'exited' status reserved for true exits per AC #4. 6. Tests: TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue (poll-interval > 0; verify loop stays alive across N empty polls); TestLoop_PreflightRejectionContinues (one bead's preflight failure doesn't abandon siblings); TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits (back-compat); TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits (back-compat). 7. Manual verification: spawn worker via 'ddx work --harness claude'; let queue drain; verify worker stays running > 5 min after queue empty (vs current behavior of immediate exit). 8. Update FEAT-006 / FEAT-010 docs noting the new long-running default + the explicit opt-out flags.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:10, area:agent, kind:fix, observed-failure</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-e34994e2" base-rev="5dad731dd53e6970742a743a54b442276ef483e8" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260502T194208-5843531b"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260502T194208-5843531b/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260502T194208-5843531b/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260502T194208-5843531b/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260502T194208-5843531b/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>
